title: Orthilia elatior
intro: Orthilia elatior, also known as tall prince's pine or tall Orthilia, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Orthilia elatior include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Orthilia elatior is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, with the most common preparations including teas for digestive issues and respiratory support.
taxonomy: Orthilia elatior belongs to the family Pyrolaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as wintergreens. This plant is a species of shrub in the genus Orthilia, characterized by its small white flowers and purple berries. Orthilia elatior is native to the mountainous regions of North America, Europe, and Asia, and is often found in moist, wooded areas. As a member of the Pyrolaceae family, Orthilia elatior is closely related to other wintergreen species, such as Pyrola and Chimaphila. The plant's classification is further supported by its unique characteristics, including its small, oval-shaped leaves and compact growth habit.
